spec_match.rationale: Anchor-scope: all on each <ul> confines --tag to that subtree. Six tags, six badges, all sharing the same anchor name — the killer test the spec was designed for.

interactive.rationale: Dropdown flips scope/unscoped class on both lists. Badges visibly relocate.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card describes 'two lists with per-row tag + badge anchors; without it every badge stacks on the first row'. Page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Scope `all` is applied at the <ul> level, not the <li> level. Within ONE list the rows still share the anchor name — and in the 'all' state the demo still shows badges stacked at the LAST row of each list. The collision is contained per-list but not per-row. The 'killer case' framing oversells what the demo actually does.

references.rationale: See also: sibling concepts only, no chromestatus.com link, no spec link. Violates CLAUDE.md rule #3.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, --accent-rose/--bg-ivory badge passes AA, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com link to see-also
openQuestions.detail: Rule #3 requires https://chromestatus.com/feature/5094192052436992 in the see-also section. Currently sibling links only.

openQuestions.title: Per-row scope, not per-list
openQuestions.detail: The blurb promises each badge attaches to its own ROW. With `anchor-scope: all` on the <ul> the three rows still share the same scope, so without unique anchor names the badges all collapse to the last row. To get per-row scope, anchor-scope should be on the <li>, not the <ul>. Either fix the CSS to scope at <li>, or update the blurb to explain this is per-list, not per-row.

openQuestions.title: Make the without-scope state clearly worse
openQuestions.detail: When the user picks 'none (collide)' it should be obvious that ALL six badges have stacked on the last row across the entire document. Add a visual cue (red outline, callout pointer) on the broken state.

summary: Correct in framing but the implementation puts anchor-scope at the WRONG level (list, not row) so the per-row promise is not delivered. Also missing the chromestatus reference link.
